texts.full_text: though we are in the age of the internet, some folks still prefer to sit down and read a magazine. Even though I own a gold prospecting website and enjoy reading information online, there’s something special about flipping through the pages of a well-written magazine dedicated to treasure hunting and prospecting. Gold Prospecting Magazines can offer you: - In-depth Articles: You get detailed articles that cover various aspects of gold prospecting, from new technologies in metal detecting to advanced panning techniques. - Community Insight: Many magazines feature stories from experienced prospectors, sharing their successes and failures. This real-world insight is invaluable for both beginners and seasoned hunters. - Equipment Reviews: Stay up-to-date with the latest equipment reviews and recommendations, helping you make informed decisions about your gear purchases. - Event Information: Learn about upcoming treasure shows, mining claims sales, and other events where you can meet fellow enthusiasts and gain more knowledge. Some of the best gold prospecting magazines include: 1. Gold & Treasure Hunter - This magazine is one of the most popular among serious prospectors. It covers a wide range of topics including metal detecting tips, historical sites, and new discoveries. 2. Lost Treasure Magazine - Focused on treasure hunting in general, this magazine also features articles on gold panning, sluicing, and other methods used to find precious metals. 3. Prospecting Today - A modern publication that emphasizes the latest trends and technologies in prospecting. It’s a great resource for those looking to stay at the cutting edge of the hobby. 4. Gold Prospector Magazine - Known for its practical advice, this magazine is tailored for individuals who are actively engaged in gold mining and exploration. 5. In particular, Na+ ions interacting with excited Rubidium atoms provide a unique probe into the population dynamics within magneto-optical trap configurations. It is shown how newly developed techniques like Magneto Optical Trap Recoil Ion Momentum Spectroscopy (MOT-RIMS) can be utilized to measure state-selective CT cross sections. This technique involves colliding an ion beam with cold, trapped atoms and analyzing scattered ions' momenta after the collision event occurs in a magneto-optical trap environment. texts.full_text: support one another in our entrepreneurial journeys. Rachel's Journey Cannabis has influenced my life physically, mentally, and spiritually. This plant inspires me on so many levels. I would not be who I am today without cannabis. But I never imagined it would become my career. yet, life has a way of bringing us the experiences we need to push us in the direction we're meant to go. Let me explain: In spring of 2015, I found myself at a major turning point. The startup I was working at was going downhill fast. Meanwhile, I was overworked and underpaid, even donating plasma just to make ends meet. To top it off, one bad event kept happening after the next. Things had reached a new low. I didn't know what direction to go... So I complained, indulged incomfort food, and wallowed in self-pity. But that never solves anything. One evening, while trying to unwind with some cannabis, I stumbled upon a documentary about the burgeoning cannabis industry.

texts.full_text: Eazy-E released music videos for "Any Last Werdz" and "Real Muthaphuckkin G's". It went multi-platinum and became one of Eazy-E's most popular recordings. Eazy-E was originally intending to release a follow up to Eazy-Duz-It named Temporary Insanity, which was going to be released as a double album. However, after the several insUlts Dr. Dre made in The Chronic, Eazy-E decided to release this EP. Dr. Dre is mentioned on every track but "Any Last Wurdz," although only indirectly, through the lyrics "D-re, I got something for you / Keep your head up high" and again in a video interview with Tupac Shakur where he says "I think that Dre is dead because his album just wasn't what it was supposed to be. He made a mistake and said some things about me in the record...and then they released another single called 'Real Muthaphuckkin G's' after that." The EP received positive reviews from critics, with AllMusic describing it as "the peak of [Eazy-E's] recording career"

texts.full_text: fact," according to FDA, three major pharmaceutical companies plan to incorporate an RFID tag into at least one of their products by the end of 2005. In its May 18 "update" of last year's report, FDA promised to play an active role in public-- and private-- sector efforts toward developing an "electronic safety net" for the drug supply, including adoption and widespread use of reliable track and trace technology by 2007. Meanwhile, the Pharmaceutical Re--search and Manufacturers Association (PhRMA) on May 13 urged a streamlined approach to help secure the U.S. pharmaceutical supply chain while a comprehensive program for use of RFID is developed. PhRMA disagreed with FDA that such a system can be in place by 2007, contending it is five years away. Thus, the association said, other short--term measures, such as establishing a paper trail "pedigree" and using barcodes on product packaging are needed. "The implementation of an RFID--based electronic pedigree system is likely to be complex and not fully achievable for five or more years," PhRMA said in a white paper on the subject. "Current use of RFID chips is limited by robustness and the impact on the affordability of medicines." "Even if stakeholders could quickly resolve the complex technological, legal, regulatory and policy issues associated with establishing a nationwide, electronic pedigree system throughout the distribution chain, there are still basic scientific issues that need to be addressed, such as the readability of RFID tags at the item, case and pallet level," the association contended. An interim solution, PhRMA said, is to focus authentication efforts at the point of manufacturer and at the pharmacy, where the drug is dispensed. "The dispensing site is a critical link in the drug distribution chain since it is the last stop before a drug is dispensed to a patient." the document said. texts.full_text: y change their picture of the political universe. Here's how Marcus puts her revelation: "Balance used to mean giving both sides equal time and space... But now balance has come too often to signify treating two unequal arguments as equals, lending legitimacy where it is not deserved." She continues with an example that should sound familiar: "When Al Gore makes a claim about his record on the environment or health care (or anything else), he's going up against what amounts essentially to political advertising from John McCain. And yet both sides are treated equally in much of 'balanced' coverage." Marcus doesn't say it outright, but she might as well have said: "It is time for journalists — including those at The Washington Post—to stop acting like dupes and hacks." Aspirin poisoning, which occurs if the dose or frequency of aspirin administration is too high, may cause vomiting and internal bleeding. If your cat shows these signs, stops eating or appears sick, aspirin therapy should be stopped and you should consult your veterinarian immediately. Cats with idiopathic cardiomyopathy (where no underlying cause is found) may remain stable for several years. Does a cat with cardiomyopathy need a special diet? texts.full_text: hat beer. Also, I want to be able to see and add ratings from multiple locations, so if I just used a file it would have to live in the cloud somewhere. Alternatively, I'd be happy to use an app on my Android phone. What's the easiest way for me to track what I've had, with information about the beers (and breweries), and still provide overall ratings without having to break them down into individual categories? Are there any websites or apps that offer this functionality similar to Beer Advocate before their recent changes? Some possible alternatives could be Untappd, RateBeer, or Brewver. These platforms allow users to rate beers with an overall score and often include features like beer descriptions, brewery information, and social sharing options. Untappd is a popular choice among craft beer enthusiasts, offering both web and mobile app access (including Android). It allows for simple check-ins of the beers you've had along with your rating. While it does encourage detailed reviews including aspects such as appearance or taste if users wish to provide them, an overall rating can be given without needing to fill out every category. RateBeer is another option that provides comprehensive beer and brewery databases alongside user ratings. It supports both simple numerical scores for beers you've tried and more in-depth critiques covering various attributes of the brews. Brewver might also fit your needs by offering a straightforward way to log what you're drinking, along with an overall rating system without requiring detailed breakdowns unless desired. Each platform has its unique features beyond just logging ratings. For instance, they often include social aspects where users can follow friends and see their reviews or join discussions about beers in forums dedicated to specific brews or breweries. Considering your requirements for cloud accessibility (to view from multiple locations), ease of use on Android devices, access to beer descriptions including categories like "winter warmer" or brewery

texts.full_text: pair of mating lions — and got more in tune with nature. See more videos from Northwestern News. The lions were just 10 feet away from our unarmed, open-air safari jeep. And they were mating. My friend Heather Moosnick (C94) and I had been searching for this amorous pair ever since we'd arrived at Xigera Camp in Botswana's Okavango Delta twenty hours earlier — an eternity when you're on the trail of wildlife, especially lions with a hankering to hug. After a tip from our guide that they were nearby and some serious searching by us for more than two days (with no luck), we'd given up hope until this moment: 10 feet away in front was one lion atop another — right there on the road, not even trying at Botswana's version of modesty. We watched as long-tail feathers sprouted from their backs and manes thickened with each thrust; they seemed oblivious to our presence despite being so close that we could see every detail through binoculars or zoom lenses

Bodhi Linux provides options to download 32-bit, 64-bit, legacy and Chromebook version. I chose 64-bit ISO, about 612 MB in size. texts.full_text: Fox News Six years ago, as the Murdoch empire was battling the outcry in the U.K. over the phone-hacking scandal involving the company’s newspapers, a former senior Fox executive wondered aloud in a private conversation: “When will it all come out?” The executive was referring to Fox News; he was certain there were things going on behind the scenes at the network’s midtown Manhattan headquarters that would spark a comparable level of public outrage once exposed. It took about five years, a generational transition among the Murdochs and a sexual harassment lawsuit filed by a fed-up female Fox News anchor to open the floodgates. During the past 10 months, Fox News and parent company 21st Century Fox have been dragged across the Rubicon by a parade of scandals, settlements and legal actions that laid bare the ugly side of the hard-charging culture fostered by Fox News founder Roger Ailes. The April 19 firing of longtime Fox News star Bill O’Reilly amounted to an even bigger sign of fundamental change than the ouster of Ailes last June. The latest in a string of controversies has seen Rupert Murdoch’s media empire grappling with allegations and internal issues that threaten its reputation. The departure or sidelining figures like Roger Ailes, Bill O’Reilly—both central to Fox News' success—and the broader context surrounding sexual harassment claims have signaled significant shifts within 21st CenturyFox. The challenges faced by FOX are not merely about individual misconduct but also reflect deeper issues of corporate culture and governance. The Murdoch family’s efforts now focus on damage control, reshaping public perception through new leadership initiatives aimed at fostering a more ethical environment while navigating the complexities posed to their media empire's integrity

texts.full_text: he protection of man and animals used in biomedical research at a United States' school of medicine. Implementation of the present regulations for the protection of humans and animals involved in biomedical research is overseen by various bodies within institutions, including Institutional Review Boards (IRBs) for human subjects and Institutional Animal Care and Use Committees (IACUCs) for animal welfare. These committees ensure that all research protocols adhere to federal guidelines set forth by agencies such as the National Institutes of Health (NIH), the Office for Human Research Protections (OHRP), and the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA). The primary goal is to safeguard both human subjects' rights, dignity, and welfare, and animal well-being through rigorous review processes that assess risk versus benefit ratios in proposed research studies. This includes ensuring informed consent from participants or their legal representatives for human subject research and implementing humane treatment standards with the least possible distress or pain when animals are used as models to advance medical science understanding at a United States' school of medicine, thereby upholding ethical principles such as respect for persons (or subjects), beneficence, non-maleficence, autonomy in decision-making

The main aims of the anaesthesiologist during any interventional neuroradiology procedure are provision...of a stable and controlled environment to minimize movement, facilitate precise positioning, and ensure adequate cerebral perfusion pressure. This includes: 1. Pre-procedure evaluation: Assessing the patient's overall health status, reviewing their medical history, and identifying potential risks associated with the procedure. 2. Monitoring vital signs: Continuously monitoring the patient's blood pressure, heart rate, oxygen saturation, and other relevant parameters to ensure stability during the procedure. 3. Management of airway: Maintaining a secure airway through endotracheal intubation or other methods as necessary, taking into account the potential for movement and discomfort during the procedure. 4. texts.full_text: ring this time that Greek and Phoenician influences appear. In the sixth century BC, Greeks occupied Corsica; the Phoenicians settled there, which can be observed through some objects and ruins that have been discovered in Ajaccio's region: pottery and remains of a Punic temple on Monte di Sori. From the second century BC to 457 AD, the Romans took control of Corsica following the capture of Cartage. During this period, Corsicans Received full Roman citizenship; when the Roman Empire fell in 476 AD, Vandals settled on the island and destroyed the ancient cities. They left about one hundred years before the Ostrogoths arrived who reigned until 534 AD. In 810 Corsica was attached to the Holy Roman Germanic Empire. During this period there were no large settlements or construction of cities; that is why, nowadays, it's difficult to find traces from those times in Ajaccio. Between 900 and 1077, Saracens settled on the island and established their dominance over the Gulf of Ajaccio, where they lived until 1425. For a while they were joined by the Byzantines; the Pisans succeeded them until 1286, when Corsica became a republic governed by the Genoese for nearly five centuries. texts.full_text: industry hierarchy—and it’s just plain rude! I mean seriously people...what happened to common courtesy? What're we doing here that makes us think ignoring potential clients or colleagues is okay?! It reflects poorly on all of our profession when some choose not even respond with a simple "No, thank you." Let alone take the time for more personalized replies. This attitude isn’t just limited to booking sessions either; it extends into how we treat each other within industry circles too—the snide comments about someone else's gear or editing style that pop up on social media threads are completely unnecesSary and uncalled-for toxicity in what should be a supportive community of artists. We all started somewhere, didn’t know everything from day one (and still don’t), so why not share knowledge instead? Why do we feel the need to tear others down rather than build them up alongside us as fellow professionals striving towards similar goals and aspirations within this creative field that brings joy into people's lives through capturing their most precious moments. So here it is: let’s be better photographers, both in our craft AND how we conduct ourselves professionally—behind the lens or keyboard!
